questions.question: Explain Virtual Circuit switching and list its three phases of communication.
keyPoints: Virtual Circuit is connection-oriented switching technique
Three phases: Connection Establishment, Data Transfer, Connection Teardown
Requires setup before data transfer
Uses VCI for packet forwarding

questions.question: What is Datagram switching? Why is it considered connectionless?
keyPoints: Datagram is connectionless switching technique
Each packet is independent
No prior setup required
Each packet carries full destination address
Packets may take different routes

questions.question: Compare Virtual Circuit and Datagram switching with respect to: (i) Connection setup (ii) Addressing (iii) Packet ordering
keyPoints: VC requires setup, Datagram does not
VC uses short VCI, Datagram uses full address
VC guarantees ordering, Datagram does not

questions.question: What is a Virtual Circuit Identifier (VCI)? Explain why VCI values change at each router.
keyPoints: VCI identifies virtual circuit at each router
VCI is locally significant
Changes at each router (label swapping)
Enables efficient forwarding

questions.question: Give two advantages and two disadvantages of Datagram switching.
keyPoints: Advantages: Resilient to failures (rerouting), Simple (no state), Scalable
Disadvantages: No ordering, Higher overhead (full address), No QoS guarantees

questions.question: Explain why Virtual Circuit switching provides better QoS support than Datagram switching.
keyPoints: VC allows resource reservation during setup
Bandwidth can be guaranteed
Traffic can be shaped and policed
Datagram has no resource reservation

questions.question: Differentiate between logical and physical addressing with examples.
keyPoints: Logical addresses (IP) are hierarchical and software-assigned
Physical addresses (MAC) are flat and hardware-based
Logical addresses used for routing
Physical addresses used for local delivery

questions.question: With the help of a diagram, explain how Virtual Circuit switching works. Also explain what happens when a router along the VC path fails.
keyPoints: Explain connection setup phase with signaling
Show VCI table maintenance at routers
Explain data transfer using VCI swapping
On router failure: All VC through that router fail
Connections must be re-established
No alternate routing possible in pure VC

questions.question: Compare Virtual Circuit and Datagram switching under the following headings: (a) Connection setup (b) Addressing overhead (c) Routing decision location (d) Quality of Service (e) Failure handling
keyPoints: VC: Setup required, DG: No setup
VC: Low overhead (VCI), DG: High overhead (full address)
VC: At connection setup time, DG: At each router
VC: Better QoS (resource reservation), DG: No QoS
VC: Entire path fails, DG: Rerouting possible

questions.question: Explain the operation of Datagram switching with a suitable diagram. Discuss why the Internet uses datagram (IP) at the network layer.
keyPoints: Explain independent packet routing
Show routing decision at each hop
Explain failure resilience and rerouting
Internet uses IP for: scalability, flexibility, independence from underlying network
Simple router design
Supports heterogeneous networks

questions.question: Discuss the concept of addressing in computer networks. Differentiate between IP addressing and MAC addressing.
keyPoints: Addressing identifies devices for communication
IP (logical) addresses: hierarchical, network + host portion
MAC (physical) addresses: flat, 48-bit unique identifier
IP used for routing across networks
MAC used for delivery within local network
NAT and DHCP for IP address management
